Clicking on the bar didn't change anything (board_changed: false). Let me think about this differently.
I've clicked all 4 white squares and they all turned gray. The level is still not complete. Let me look at the purple pattern more carefully. Maybe I need to click on the purple dots in the bottom-left panel, not the white squares in the bottom-right grid.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the game mechanics. Looking at the bottom-left panel:
```
GGGGGGGGGG
GgggppgggG
GgggppgggG
GggggggggG
GppggggppG
GppggggppG
GggggggggG
GgggppgggG
GgggppgggG
```
The purple dots form a pattern. Maybe I need to click on the purple dots to "activate" them, and this changes the bottom-right grid.
Let me try clicking on a purple dot.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find purple nodes in the bottom-left panel
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'p':
        print(f"Purple node {n['id']}: {n['pixels']} pixels, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
